sections.paragraph:
Ἐμμανουήλ, ὁ indecl. (עִמָּנוּ אֵל Is 7:14; 8:8 both Mt; Is 7:14 LXX; TestSol; GrBar 4:15 (Christ). Greek Christians spell it Ἐμμανουήλ: Recueil des inscriptions grecques chrétiennes d’Égypte, ed. GLefebvre 1907, 214; 222; POxy 1162, 14 [IV a.d.]; PUps 8 verso) Emmanuel. In a quot. of Is 7:14 applied to Jesus Mt 1:23 and defined as μεθʼ ἡμῶν ὁ θεός God w. us.—HWildberger, Jesaja I, ’72, 263 (lit.); BHHW II 761. DDD ‘Emmanuel’ 572f.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PUps 8 PUps 8 = PUpsaliensis 8—List 4

quot. quot. = quotation

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

DDD DDD = Dictionary of Deities and Demons in the Bible—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 322
